summary: This week in AI, significant advancements and investments have been made in various sectors, including healthcare, education, and technology. OpenAI's potential launch of a $2B AI drug discovery startup and Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI have made headlines.
insight: The increasing trend of AI adoption in diverse industries highlights the need for standardized regulations and ethical considerations to ensure responsible AI development and deployment.
highlights: OpenAI researcher Miles Wang is in talks to launch a $2B AI drug discovery startup, indicating growing investor interest in AI applications in life sciences.
Apple has sued OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft, raising concerns about intellectual property protection in the AI industry.
New York has become the first state to impose a moratorium on new data center construction, citing environmental and energy consumption concerns related to AI infrastructure.

articles.title: OpenAI researcher Miles Wang in talks to launch AI drug discovery startup valued at $2B
articles.description: The funding discussions point to investor interest in applying AI to make breakthroughs in life sciences.

articles.title: OpenAI pushes back on Apple trade secret lawsuit
articles.description: OpenAI has issued another statement on the lawsuit, this time suggesting it lacks merit.

articles.title: Lawsuit claims Meta's layoff decisions were made by AI, not humans
articles.description: Meta denies using AI to terminate workers with disabilities and medical problems.

articles.title: Google faces another AI training lawsuit from major publishers
articles.description: Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, and other publishers allege that Google trained its AI on copyrighted works without the necessary permissions.

articles.title: DeepMind CEO calls for an independent standards body to regulate frontier AI
articles.description: DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis is proposing an AI "standards body" modeled after FINRA, to test frontier models and develop best practices for their release.

articles.title: Nemotron Labs: How Open Models Give Enterprises and Nations AI They Can Trust, Control and Customize

articles.title: New York State halts construction of all new data centers

articles.title: Reflection inks $1B compute deal with Nebius
articles.description: Reflection AI has signed a $1 billion deal to access Nebius' compute. Reflection was founded in 2024 and is developing open source AI technology.

articles.title: Apple sues OpenAI after ex-engineer allegedly used bug to steal trade secrets
articles.description: OpenAI accused of conspiring with former Apple employees to steal trade secrets.

articles.title: How Deutsche Telekom is rewiring telecommunications with AI
articles.description: How Deutsche Telekom is becoming an AI-native telco with OpenAI-transforming customer service, employee workflows, network operations, and the future of voice.
